pages.full_text:
east past Rheims. 6.50. we reached huremont et Villotte, a nice quiet campsite with 2 dogs, ducks + chickens. Showers in the day. A bit of sun in the evening. We walked down to look at the canal.
__June 8th__
We cycled along the canal + down to the river + up through the modern village. Set of in van 9.45.
We shopped, or, rather, Simon shopped while I read. We had a good lunch at a restaurant by a river in __Gray__ which Simon had visited twice before with Charles.
We reached __Pesmes__ a very pretty town with winding streets + the remains of a Roman layout at the top. After supper we wandered around the town. V. hilly.
__June 9th Sunday:__
We again wandered around the town in a different part. St. Hilaire's the church, [has no service. today, Whitsun. Instead,]
pages.notes: Top of page is a continuation from the previous spread — text begins mid-sentence 'east past Rheims'. No handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner. The campsite name reads 'huremont et Villotte' — could also be 'Huremont et Villotte' (proper noun). The last line is partly cut off at the bottom of the image; text in brackets is a best reading of the partially visible line. Year cannot be determined from the page itself; June 9th falling on a Sunday is consistent with multiple years within 2003–2019 (e.g. 2002 does not fit; candidate years: 2013, 2019).

pages.full_text:
Notre-Dame de Gray Basilica

View from our campsite, across the river at __Pesmes__ on R. Ognon.
photo_captions: View from our campsite, across the river at Pesmes on R. Ognon.
pages.notes: Page contains two glued-in photos with handwritten captions. Top photo shows a church/basilica building (Notre-Dame de Gray Basilica). Bottom photo shows a town viewed from across a river (Pesmes on R. Ognon). The handwritten page number '23' is partially visible in the top-right corner. 'Pesmes' is underlined in the caption. 'R. Ognon' is the River Ognon in the Franche-Comté region of France.
